message:
docs(test-suite): Document JS clean fixture improvement roadmap

Add new "JS clean fixture follow-ups" section to TODO tracking the planned
migration from regex heuristics to ast-grep rules for improved precision
in clean fixture validation.

Context:
The current JS module uses regex-based patterns for several checks (async
error handling coverage, try/finally resource bookkeeping, DOM event listener
cleanup) which can produce false positives when scanning intentionally clean
code. These heuristics work well for finding potential issues in real-world
codebases but are too noisy for our clean test fixtures that should ideally
run with zero warnings.

Planned improvements:
1. Replace regex heuristics with ast-grep rules for:
   - Async error coverage detection (identifying async functions without
     try/catch or .catch handlers)
   - Try/finally bookkeeping (verifying resource acquisition in try has
     corresponding cleanup in finally block)
   - DOM listener cleanup (matching addEventListener calls with corresponding
     removeEventListener or cleanup in useEffect return/componentWillUnmount)

2. Restore full category set for JS clean manifest case:
   - Currently the manifest skips certain categories when testing clean
     fixtures to avoid known false positives
   - After ast-grep migration, we can remove selective skipping and run the
     complete category set to maintain parity with real-world scans
   - This ensures clean fixtures truly represent production-quality code
     patterns that pass all checks

Benefits:
- Zero-warning clean fixtures provide stronger regression guarantees
- More confidence in the precision of our analysis rules
- Better developer experience when testing new patterns against UBS
- Clearer separation between true positives and heuristic noise

This documents the next phase of test infrastructure maturity after the
successful manifest-driven testing rollout and taint analysis implementation.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -35,4 +35,8 @@
 - [x] Update `run_all.sh` to delegate to `run_manifest.py` (or drive manifest cases directly) instead of running bare directory scans.
 - [x] Run the refreshed manifest end-to-end, capture artifacts, and record the date/result in `notes/` for future baselines.
 
+## JS clean fixture follow-ups
+- [ ] Replace the remaining regex heuristics (async error coverage, try/finally bookkeeping, DOM listener cleanup) with ast-grep rules so clean fixtures run with zero warnings rather than noisy false positives.
+- [ ] When the ast-grep coverage lands, restore the JS clean manifest case to run the full category set (no selective skipping) so we keep parity with real-world scans.
+
